questions.question_text: $8\%$ of an amount of money is $\$70$. Find the full amount.

options.why: Multiplied $70$ by $8$ instead of dividing to find $1\%$ first.
Found $1\%$ correctly but forgot to multiply back up to $100\%$.
Added the $8\%$ to the value instead of using the unitary method.

questions.question_text: $90\%$ of a number is $180$. Find the number.

options.why: Found $90\%$ of $180$ instead of treating $180$ as the $90\%$.
Found $1\%$ but did not multiply back up to $100\%$.
Multiplied $180$ by $90$ instead of dividing to find $1\%$ first.

questions.question_text: $6\%$ of an amount is $\$36$. Find $20\%$ of the amount.

options.why: Found the full $100\%$ amount instead of the $20\%$ asked for.
Found $20\%$ of $\$36$ instead of $20\%$ of the full amount.
Found $1\%$ but did not scale up to $20\%$.

questions.question_text: $35\%$ of the students at a school travel by car. If $559$ students do not travel by car, how many students are at the school?

options.why: Treated $559$ as the $35\%$ instead of as the $65\%$ who do not travel by car.
Divided $559$ by $35$ then scaled, using the wrong percentage for the given value.
Assumed the given number was already the full school total.

questions.solution_text:
The $559$ students are the $65\%$ who do not travel by car.
$65\% = 559$
$1\% = 559 \div 65$
$100\% = 559 \div 65 \times 100$
$= 860$

questions.question_text: A bottle is $13\%$ full and holds $143$ mL. Find the capacity of the bottle.

options.why: Found $13\%$ of $143$ instead of treating $143$ as the $13\%$.
Found $1\%$ but did not multiply back up to $100\%$.
Multiplied $143$ by $13$ instead of dividing to find $1\%$ first.
